title: APT Cheat Sheet
description: Essential APT commands for package management on Debian-based systems.

commands.description: Update the package list to get information on the newest versions of packages and their dependencies.
commands.scenario: Update the package list to get information on the newest versions of packages and their dependencies.

commands.description: Upgrade all the packages to the latest version available.
commands.scenario: Upgrade all the packages to the latest version available.

commands.description: Install a new package by name.
commands.scenario: Install a new package by name.

commands.description: Show details about a specific package.
commands.scenario: Show details about a specific package.

commands.description: Search for a package in the package list.
commands.scenario: Search for a package in the package list.

commands.description: Remove a package but keep its configuration files.
commands.scenario: Remove a package but keep its configuration files.

commands.description: Remove a package and its configuration files.
commands.scenario: Remove a package and its configuration files.

commands.description: Clean up the local repository of retrieved package files.
commands.scenario: Clean up the local repository of retrieved package files.

commands.description: Remove packages that were automatically installed to satisfy dependencies for other packages and are no longer needed.
commands.scenario: Remove packages that were automatically installed to satisfy dependencies for other packages and are no longer needed.

commands.description: Upgrade the system by installing/upgrading packages.
commands.scenario: Upgrade the system by installing/upgrading packages.

commands.description: Performs a more intelligent upgrade than 'upgrade' by handling changing dependencies in new versions of packages, allowing for the removal of obsolete packages if necessary.
Uses advanced pattern matching to search for specific states; '~i' filters for installed packages, allowing for precise dependency and state auditing.
Displays a list of all packages that currently have available updates without performing the actual upgrade process.
Uses 'mark' to set a package to 'hold' status, preventing it from being upgraded during a system-wide upgrade to maintain environment stability.
Shows the installation candidate, the version currently installed, and the priority/source of all available versions in the configured repositories.
